title: Protecting the Dignity of Unborn Children Act of 2025
short_title: Protecting the Dignity of Unborn Children Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes it a federal crime to carelessly throw away or abandon fetal remains in landfills or waterways. Fetal remains means any part of a fetus after an abortion, except for cremated parts. People who break this law can be fined or sent to prison for up to three years. However, women cannot be charged with this crime for disposing of remains from their own pregnancies.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protecting the Dignity of Unborn Children Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibiting Unlawful Disposal of Fetal Remains
sections.text: This section adds a new chapter to the federal criminal code. The new chapter makes it a crime to carelessly throw away or leave behind fetal remains in a landfill or in any waterway that can be used for shipping. Fetal remains means any part of an unborn baby that died because of an abortion, but does not include parts that have been cremated. A landfill means a place where garbage is buried in the ground following health and safety rules. Navigable waters means rivers, lakes, and other bodies of water that boats can use to travel. Anyone who breaks this law can be put in prison for up to three years or fined, or both. However, this law cannot be used to charge a woman for disposing of the remains of her own unborn child.
